questions: Q1: Does the content module accurately document all CLI commands and flags?
Q2: Is the package.json exports map correctly configured?
Q3: Is the content module browser-safe for Vite bundling?

answers.answer: Yes. All 13 CLI command source files were cross-referenced against the documentation. Every documented flag matches the source code exactly. No undocumented flags were added, and no source-code flags were omitted from the documentation. The content includes all six ticket-required worked examples plus two additional high-value patterns.
evidence: src/tickets/create.ts: all 5 flags match (--title, --description, --description-file, --repos, --mode) with correct mode values
src/tickets/get.ts: --json flag and ref resolution (internal ID, short ID, number) confirmed
src/tickets/update-description.ts: --file, --text flags confirmed as mutually exclusive
src/org/index.ts: current, list, switch subcommands confirmed

answers.answer: Yes. The exports field has a './docs' entry with 'types' condition first (TypeScript convention) pointing to './dist/docs/cli-content.d.ts' and 'import' condition pointing to './dist/docs/cli-content.js'. Only 'import' is needed since the package is ESM-only (type: module). The existing 'bin' field is unaffected by the exports addition.
evidence: package.json lines 9-14: exports field with correct structure
dist/docs/cli-content.js (8063 bytes) and cli-content.d.ts (141 bytes) exist

answers.answer: Yes. The source file (src/docs/cli-content.ts) contains zero import statements — it is a pure 'export const' declaration. The compiled output (dist/docs/cli-content.js) contains no require() calls or Node.js built-in module references. The module is safe for Vite browser bundling.
evidence: grep for 'import' in src/docs/cli-content.ts: no matches
grep for require() and Node built-ins in dist/docs/cli-content.js: no matches
npm run build in helix-global-client (which bundles via Vite): exit 0

statement: Code review of helix-cli found no issues. The canonical content module is a correct, browser-safe, pure-data TypeScript module with all documented flags verified against 13 CLI source files. The package.json exports map is correctly configured. All quality gates pass. No code changes made by review.
